// ==== Cargo.toml ====
# <auto-generated/>
[package]
name = "koine-domain"
version = "0.0.0"
edition = "2021"
description = "Generated by Koine — do not edit by hand."

[dependencies]
rust_decimal = "1"
regex = "1"

[lib]
path = "src/lib.rs"

// ==== src/billing.rs ====
// <auto-generated/>
//! The `Billing` bounded context.

use crate::koine_runtime::Decimal;

/// The errors a domain operation in this context can fail with.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum DomainError {
    /// An invariant of `type_name` was violated: `rule`.
    InvariantViolation { type_name: &'static str, rule: &'static str },
    /// Two quantities of different units were combined.
    UnitMismatch { type_name: &'static str },
}

impl std::fmt::Display for DomainError {
    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
        match self {
            DomainError::InvariantViolation { type_name, rule } => write!(f, "{type_name}: {rule}"),
            DomainError::UnitMismatch { type_name } => write!(f, "{type_name}: units do not match"),
        }
    }
}

impl std::error::Error for DomainError {}

/// A monetary amount in a currency. Never negative.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub struct Money {
    amount: Decimal,
    currency: Currency,
}

impl Money {
    /// Creates a validated `Money`, running its invariants.
    pub fn new(amount: Decimal, currency: Currency) -> Result<Self, DomainError> {
        if !(amount >= Decimal::from(0i64)) {
            return Err(DomainError::InvariantViolation { type_name: "Money", rule: "a monetary amount cannot be negative" });
        }
        Ok(Self {
            amount,
            currency,
        })
    }

    pub fn amount(&self) -> Decimal { self.amount }

    pub fn currency(&self) -> Currency { self.currency }
}

/// Currencies, carrying their symbol and minor-unit count.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
pub enum Currency {
    Eur,
    Usd,
    Gbp,
}

impl Currency {
    pub fn symbol(&self) -> &'static str {
        match self {
            Currency::Eur => "€",
            Currency::Usd => "$",
            Currency::Gbp => "£",
        }
    }

    pub fn decimals(&self) -> i64 {
        match self {
            Currency::Eur => 2,
            Currency::Usd => 2,
            Currency::Gbp => 2,
        }
    }
}

/// An email address, shape-validated.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub struct Email {
    raw: String,
}

impl Email {
    /// Creates a validated `Email`, running its invariants.
    pub fn new(raw: String) -> Result<Self, DomainError> {
        if !(crate::koine_runtime::regex_is_match(r"^[^@]+@[^@]+$", &raw)) {
            return Err(DomainError::InvariantViolation { type_name: "Email", rule: "invalid email address" });
        }
        Ok(Self {
            raw,
        })
    }

    pub fn raw(&self) -> &str { &self.raw }
}

// ==== src/koine_runtime.rs ====
// <auto-generated/>
//! Shared runtime support for the generated domain crate. Hand-edits will be overwritten.

#![allow(dead_code)]

/// Money-safe fixed-point decimal. Re-exported so generated modules can `use
/// crate::koine_runtime::Decimal;` without naming the backing crate.
pub use rust_decimal::Decimal;

/// A point in time. Aliased to the standard wall-clock type so the crate stays dependency-light.
pub type Instant = std::time::SystemTime;

/// The current instant (the Koine `now` value builtin).
pub fn now() -> Instant {
    std::time::SystemTime::now()
}

/// Parses a decimal literal at its exact textual form (money-safe, never via `f64`).
pub fn dec(text: &str) -> Decimal {
    Decimal::from_str_exact(text).expect("invalid decimal literal")
}

/// A closed interval `[start, end]` over an ordered type — the Koine `Range<T>`.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
pub struct Range<T: PartialOrd> {
    start: T,
    end: T,
}

impl<T: PartialOrd> Range<T> {
    /// Creates an interval. The caller is responsible for `start <= end` (a domain invariant).
    pub fn new(start: T, end: T) -> Self {
        Self { start, end }
    }

    pub fn start(&self) -> &T {
        &self.start
    }

    pub fn end(&self) -> &T {
        &self.end
    }

    /// True when `value` lies within the closed interval.
    pub fn contains(&self, value: &T) -> bool {
        *value >= self.start && *value <= self.end
    }

    /// True when this interval and `other` share at least one point.
    pub fn overlaps(&self, other: &Range<T>) -> bool {
        self.start <= other.end && other.start <= self.end
    }
}

/// Unanchored regex test (the Koine `matches /pattern/` operator). A malformed pattern — which
/// the compiler's regex validation rejects upstream — conservatively yields `false`.
pub fn regex_is_match(pattern: &str, input: &str) -> bool {
    regex::Regex::new(pattern).map(|re| re.is_match(input)).unwrap_or(false)
}

/// Decimal-safe sum over a non-empty iterator (the Koine `.sum(...)` fold). Panics on an empty
/// input, mirroring the seedless-fold behaviour of the C#/TypeScript/Python emitters.
pub fn koine_sum<T>(items: impl IntoIterator<Item = T>) -> T
where
    T: std::ops::Add<Output = T>,
{
    items
        .into_iter()
        .reduce(|acc, x| acc + x)
        .expect("sum of an empty collection")
}

/// The minimum of a non-empty iterator (the Koine `.min(...)` fold).
pub fn koine_min<T: PartialOrd>(items: impl IntoIterator<Item = T>) -> T {
    items
        .into_iter()
        .reduce(|acc, x| if x < acc { x } else { acc })
        .expect("min of an empty collection")
}

/// The maximum of a non-empty iterator (the Koine `.max(...)` fold).
pub fn koine_max<T: PartialOrd>(items: impl IntoIterator<Item = T>) -> T {
    items
        .into_iter()
        .reduce(|acc, x| if x > acc { x } else { acc })
        .expect("max of an empty collection")
}

// ==== src/lib.rs ====
// <auto-generated/>
//! Generated domain crate. Hand-edits will be overwritten.

pub mod koine_runtime;
pub mod billing;
